batches.name: Batch #115
batches.get: Smokers who bought fake mullein and it failed. Smokers shopping for mullein right now.
batches.who: they're being lied to by the mullein market and they don't know it. Green droppers that are 80% chlorophyll dye. Gummies that are candy. Capsules that dissolve in stomach acid. Tea bags killed by boiling water. They're spending $30 a bottle on products that never reach their lungs. Some of them already tried these products and gave up on mullein entirely — thinking mullein doesn't work. It wasn't mullein that failed. It was the delivery method and the filler.
batches.to: Realize that every mullein product they've seen, bought, or considered is fake. Then discover the one version that's actually real  concentrated, pure, amber not green, sublingual delivery that bypasses the stomach and reaches the lungs directly.
batches.by: The exposé format systematically destroys each product category — green droppers, gummies, capsules, tea bags — by naming what's actually inside them and linking those ingredients to disgusting non-supplement comparisons. The viewer is repulsed out of every other option. Origin Drops arrives at the end as the only clean alternative. The product isn't pitched. It's revealed as the answer to everything that was just exposed
batches.purchaseTrigger: Everything I've been buying is fake. The green is dye. The gummy is candy. The capsule never reaches my lungs. This is the only real one."
batches.beliefBefore: Mullein is mullein. Any brand works. I'll buy whatever's cheapest.
batches.beliefAfter: Most mullein products are fake. The color, the form, the delivery — it all matters. Only one version is real
batches.hypothesis: This is a variation on a past comparison rank and Ad winner.  We are testing if this new style format is going to be our comparison ranking ad.
batches.script:
Fake mullein is finally being exposed. Millions of versions of mullein are being sold on TikTok and Amazon claiming to clear smokers' lungs. Almost none of them contain what you think.
Smokers are taking mullein expecting it to clear the junk out of their lungs. But most of these products barely contain a functional dose. The market is flooded with mullein gummies that are mostly sugar and gelatin with a trace of mullein so small it does nothing. Mullein capsules make bold claims but they're packed with rice flour and fillers. You swallow them. Your stomach acid destroys them in minutes. Whatever mullein was inside gets absorbed into your gut. Not your lungs. Your lungs never see it. That dark green dropper all over TikTok? That green isn't mullein. That's chlorophyll dye. Researchers at MD Anderson found zero proven lung benefits. And mullein tea bags? Boiling water destroys the active compounds before you even sip it. You'd need eight cups a day to get a real dose.
That's when a small independent company decided to take matters into their own hands. Origin Drops refused to cut corners. They made a 100% pure concentrated mullein liquid. No chlorophyll. No fillers. No sugar. No rice flour. You put it under your tongue. It bypasses your stomach completely and goes straight to the lungs. They blended it with 8 supporting herbs and made it 20 times stronger than anything else on the market. Over 10,000 smokers have already switched. They sell out regularly because they produce in small batches to keep the potency high. 30-day money back guarantee. Link below if it's still in stock.

batches.name: Batch #114
batches.get: Smokers 35-65 who respond to strength and potency claims. The same audience buying from the current winner. People who've tried weak mullein and want the strongest version available.
batches.who: They smoke. Their lungs are full. They've seen mullein everywhere but most of it looked weak. They want something aggressive. Something that actually DOES something. They don't want gentle. They want strong. The current winning ad already proved this is what makes them click.
batches.to: Reinforce that Origin Drops is so strong it's almost too much. The strength that made them buy in the first ad gets confirmed through a real customer reaction in this ad. The result isn't claimed. It's complained about.
batches.by: he fake complaint format. The customer says the mullein was "uncontrollably strong." They coughed up dark junk. Their shortness of breath vanished. They're terrified to take it again. But they're ordering three more bottles. Every complaint is actually a proof point for the strength angle that's already winning.
batches.purchaseTrigger: Oh, so this mullein is the strongest mullein.
batches.beliefBefore: Every mullein I've tried was weak. Nothing came out. Nothing changed. They're probably all the same.
batches.beliefAfter: This one is apparently so strong people are coughing up 20-year-old tar and complaining about it. That's the opposite of everything else I've tried."
batches.hypothesis: The winning "strongest mullein" angle proved smokers buy based on potency. A winning angle. This iteration delivers the same strength message through a fake complaint format  in a negative marketing.
batches.script:
I don't want this mullein anymore. I want a refund immediately."
"Sorry to hear that. Can I ask why?"
"This mullein is uncontrollably strong. After my first dose it made me cough up dark thick junk into the sink that I haven't seen come out of me in years. Now I'm terrified of taking it again."
"So you want to stop?"
"No. Send me three more bottles. But you need to warn people."

loops.hypothesisWhy:
Hypothesis 1: The symptom list targets too many avatars (allergy sufferers, elderly, overweight, general congestion) instead of going deep on the core smoker. This inflates CTR with unqualified clicks that never convert — high curiosity, low purchase intent.
Hypothesis 2: The smoker — the actual buyer — only hears themselves in 2 out of 8 pain points, so the ad doesn't feel personally written for them. They don't feel seen enough to build the conviction needed to purchase.

Three, I did this ad, probably variation, a lot of times. The first time I did it was eyes, and you completely flopped. Do we need to just X this concept out?
